firstNightReminder: Dusk
otherNightReminder: Dusk
name: Dusk

firstNightReminder: Dawn
otherNightReminder: Dawn
name: Dawn

otherNightReminder: If the Blabber’s public statement was completely true about the game state with regards to the Blabber character: Choose a player not protected from dying tonight. That player dies.
name: Blabber
ability: Each day, you may make a public *game-related* statement. Tonight, if it was true *by registration*, a player dies.

otherNightReminder: The Faustian points to 1 player and a character on their sheet and/or gives a "good" signal (no "evil" signal). If the guess is *correct*, add the "Faust Kill" :reminder: to the player. If *wrong*, add the "Faust Kill" :reminder: to the Faustian. Later, show the Demon that the Faustian chose the player(s) with "Faust Kill" reminder. Then, if the Demon chooses the Faustian when using their ability, the Demon ability ignores the Faustian. Otherwise, if the player with "Faust Kill" reminder *could* die from the Demon, the Demon automatically replaces 1 choice to attack this player and the Faustian accounts for choosing this player. Before dawn, remove the "Faust Kill" :reminder:s. The Faustian affects themself if they have also a Demon ability. In this case, waking up again can be skipped, it happens automatically.
name: Faustian
ability: Each night*, you guess character and/or good alignment of 1 alive player. If right, their death – else your death – replaces 1 Demon choice & you account for it.

otherNightReminder: Remove the "Protected" :reminder:s, "Drunk" :reminder:s and "Deranged" :reminder:s of the Adulterator. The Adulterator points to 1 or 2 players and may choose themself as well. Add the "Protected" :reminder: to both. Add the "Drunk" :reminder: to one of both. Add the "Deranged" :reminder: to the same player or the other one. Players with "Protected" reminder don't die *tonight* (if they have the "Drunk" reminder) and don't die *until next dusk* (if they have the "Deranged" reminder). Players with "Drunk" reminder are drunk *tonight*. Players with "Deranged" reminder are deranged *until next dusk*.
name: Adulterator
ability: Each night*, choose 1 or 2 players (incl. yourself): they can't die tonight, 1 is drunk. *1 is deranged and can't die until dusk.*

otherNightReminder: Let the soulmate choose at most 1 person after demon but before minions. When the soulmate chose, it is like the demon tried to kill them. Independent of the success of death, this soulmate loses *all* abilities. At the onset of the vessel effect – as soon as the vessel dies – tell them that the Soulmate chose them. (Do this even if they chose themself.)
name: Soulmate
ability: Each night*, choose ≤ 1 person who has their abilities when dead & triggers no effects on death. When you chose you die and lose *all* abilities! Fixed alignment!

firstNightReminder: Do not inform the Demon/Minions who each other are but prepare 3 bluffs independently for each evil person. The bluffs may overlap.
otherNightReminder: If the Poppy Grower has died, show the Minions/Demon who each other are.
name: Poppy Snitch
ability: Minions & Demons do not know each other *but get 3 independent bluffs*. If you die, minions & demons get to know each other that night.

firstNightReminder: Choose a character token that the Puzzledrunk should have and see initially. Put it into the bag, not the Puzzledrunk character. This allows you to put up to one illegal pair of characters in the bag. The Puzzledrunk player will play this character but it's ability will always be drunk. Add the "Puzzled" :reminder: to this player. Also choose a player who may guess the Puzzledrunk. Add the "Puzzler" :reminder: to them. Wake that player to inform them that the Puzzledrunk is in play.
otherNightReminder: At most the player with "Puzzler" reminder may guess the Puzzledrunk. You should refuse an answer to them, if they are the wrong player. If they guessed publicly or privately, privately tell them a player name. If their guess is right, you give a correct player name, else an incorrect one (but not the name of the asking player).
name: Puzzledrunk
ability: You have another drunk character. Once per game, 1 good player may guess you. If right, they learn the *actual* Demon player, else get false info (not themself).

firstNightReminder: Choose 1 person to whom you add unknown rules and modifications whose effect holds while this person has their abilities. Choose at most the regular number of evil players to let them always register as Minions + 1 Demon to other abilities. You may fake the public or private Minion signals that the Minion abilities emit. Choose 1 demon ability for yourself. Nothing else is manipulated.
otherNightReminder: Remain consist and don't manipulate more than you are allowed to. If the manipulated player has no ability anymore, their manipulation is gone. When the game ends, explain what manipulation and rules you have chosen.
name: Rationalist
ability: You are Atheist. The Story Teller modifies ≤ 1 player's abilities, misregisters ≤ X players as evil minions/demons, fakes minion signals & has 1 demon ability. [X = regular # of evil]

firstNightReminder: See Other Night Reminder
otherNightReminder: Add the "psyched" :reminder: to any 2 *other* players who choose, see or learn about the Psychic player. If there are less than 2, add additional "psyched" :reminder:s such that there are two players. At the end of the night, wake the Psychic and point to all players with "psyched" reminder. Remove the "psyched" :reminder:s.
name: Psychic
ability: Each dawn, you see 2 players. Among them are all but ≤ 2 players whose abilities interacted with you at night.

firstNightReminder: Determine an ability or discernible modification for a minion character that you want to give to the Sectarian. (It must work differently from their character token.) Give correct minion info to the Sectarian and treat the Sectarian as minion for evil players. Treat the Sectarian as evil Townsfolk or Outsider for good players.
otherNightReminder: Play the special ability of the Sectarian which is different from their seen minion character. The Sectarian registers as evil minion to evil players and as evil Townsfolk or Outsider to good players.
name: Sectarian
ability: You register as evil minion to yourself and evil players & as evil non-minion to other players. You are good & your minion is not your real ability.

firstNightReminder: Add the "instigated" reminder to the (dead or alive) player to the left and to the right of the Instigator. From now on, the instigated players are deranged until next dusk. Optional: only make one player deranged. Add the reminder "CW" (clockwise only) or "CCW" (counterclockwise only) reminder.
otherNightReminder: At dusk, move the "instigated" reminder to the X-th clockwise and counterclockwise (dead or alive) neighbour of the Instigator. The instigated players are deranged until next dusk. Optional: only make one player deranged, clockwise if "CW" reminder is set, counterclockwise if "CCW" is set.
name: Instigator
ability: On night/day X, your X-th neighbour (clockwise AND counterclockwise) is deranged.
attribution: image: https://www.flaticon.com/free-icons/domino – Domino icons created by nangicon - Flaticon

firstNightReminder: Show the character of 1 demon player to the Antifascist. Rest: See Other Night Reminder.
otherNightReminder: Show the number of alive good plus evil players who *register as being of Minion type* to the Antifascist.
name: Antifascist
ability: You start knowing the character of 1 Demon player. Each night, you learn the number of alive minions.

firstNightReminder: Determine a valuable *actually good* Townsfolk (not the Bodyguard) or dangerous Outsider and add the "VIP" reminder to them. I.e. the VIP's actual alignment is good even if they misregister. they win  Wake up the Demon. Show them the Bodyguard chose them, point at the player with "VIP" reminder and show the VIP's character. At the end of the night, wake up the Bodyguard and point to the VIP player.
otherNightReminder: If a new player got a Demon ability, show that the Bodyguard chose them, point at the VIP player and the VIP's character. If the player with VIP reminder was killed by the Demon, wake up the Bodyguard and point to the Demon player. After dawn, if the VIP died tonight, declare the character in addition to the VIP having died.
name: Bodyguard
ability: You start knowing the *actually good* VIP. You learn the Demon – who knows VIP+character – if they kill the VIP. VIP's character is declared if they die, even if you died.

firstNightReminder: See Other Night Reminder
otherNightReminder: Let the Biologist choose 1 player (you may allow them to choose themself). If the player has a character in the Demon section of the script, you may give a "not minion" answer. If the character is from the minion section, you may give a "not demon" answer. Otherwise, the answer is arbitrary. Alternatively, you may answer "left" (if in the left column) or "right" (if in the right column).
name: Biologist
ability: Each night, choose 1 player: You learn whether their character is either *not* in the Minion section, *not* in the Demon section, "right" or "left" in the script.

otherNightReminder: Let the Necrophage choose a player (including themself). If this player's life state *may* or *might* register differently in different contexts/game rules, give a "no" answer, else a "yes" answer.
name: Necrophage
ability: Each night*, choose 1 player. You learn if their public AND covert life state registers correctly.

firstNightReminder: Find a common game-related property that at least 2 players should have in this game. Show the first player to the Logician tonight.
otherNightReminder: Show another player to the Logician who satisfies the same game state condition as the previously seen players at their corresponding night.
name: Logician
ability: Each night, you see 1 player different from last night. All seen players have something in common at the time of seeing them.

firstNightReminder: See Other Night Reminder
otherNightReminder: At dusk, if the Kindergardener has no "protection" reminder, remove all "Kinder-sick" :reminder:s. If the Kindergardener has the "protection" reminder tonight, add the "Kinder-sick" reminder to each player who otherwise would have died tonight after the first dying player. If a "Kinder-sick" reminder is added to a player, the player does not die & 1 "Kinder-sick" :reminder:  is added to the Kindergardener (if not already present). Players with "Kinder-sick" reminder have the sick state (they don't wake up for their abilities and cannot nominate). Now, if the Kindergardener has no "Kinder-sick" reminder, let them choose 1 player. Add the "child" :reminder: to that player. If the player with "child" reminder has died or dies tonight or the next day, then add the "protection" :reminder: to the Kindergardener. If the Kindergardener has the "Kinder-sick" reminder, give them 1 info signal (character, character type, alignment, player or player state) for each other player with "Kinder-sick" reminder. Don't tell which signal is associated to which player (except when the signal is the player). Remove the "protection" :reminder: afterwards.
name: Kindergardener
ability: Each night, choose someone: if they die in this cycle, the next night's 2nd, 3rd,… victim doesn't die & get sick with you. Get 1 info for each of them, even if sick.

otherNightReminder: Add a "killed" :reminder: to each dying player and a "protected" :reminder: to each player whose death is prevented by protection. Wake the Pastor (or let them visit you the next day). They show you one question whose answer must directly concern info about the players who either died or did not due to protection since last dawn. The question may not make or ask for assumptions about anything else than player alignment or character type, else you either give an empty answer (a maybe) or request a new question. The paster may ask for any 1 info signal directly & not more than 1 info signal.
name: Pastor
ability: Each night*, ask for 1 info signal concerning tonight's or yesterday's death targets. (The info can only be conditional regarding alignment or type of others.)

firstNightReminder: Choose a not-in-play Townsfolk for the Gargoyle or let a dedicated Gargoyle player choose one *before the game*. In night 1, the Gargoyle only has the Gargoyle character.
otherNightReminder: Even if the Village Gargoyle player is dead, the Village Gargoyle character still lives (and their Townsfolk character *might* as well). The first time on night ≥ 2, if the team of the Village Gargoyle has more alive players than the other team(s), the Village Gargoyle dies at night, *publicly*(!), and the Demon skips their action of that night. Additionally: add a Townsfolk character by adding its token to the Village Gargoyle token in the Grimoire. The first time on night ≥ 2 when the Village Gargoyle is not part of the majority team *anymore*, wake them to choose 1 other (dead or alive) player. The Village Gargoyle's Townsfolk character moves to the chosen player whose previous character is replaced. If it is a Demon player with active ability, yet another *alive* player becomes the evil Demon of equal charater instead.
name: Village Gargoyle
ability: On night ≥ 2, when your team has majority, you are demon-killed & get a not-in-play Townsfolk. Once your team loses it, choose 1 player to get your Townsfolk. Your character lives even if dead.

firstNightReminder: Choose a demon character for the Maniac and put it into the bag for the Maniac to draw. The Maniac gets Demon info. All info of the Maniac is strictly false. During the Demon info of the real Demon, show them what Demon character the Maniac has. (Don't show the player!!)
otherNightReminder: Play the deranged Demon ability of the Maniac. Add a "maniac-ed" :reminder: to each player the Maniac chooses. A deranged ability changes player states in the opposite way. E.g. instead of killing players, the Maniac might protect them *from 1 kill*, add 1 life, resurrect 1, etc. Instead of poisoning players, they would be protected against 1 source of poisoning for some time. Inform the Demon about the players with "maniac-ed" reminder. Later tonight, remove all "maniac-ed" :reminder:s.
name: Maniac
ability: You think to be a Demon, register as Demon & have a deranged Demon ability. The Demon knows your character & who you choose.

otherNightReminder: Before the Demon the Balloonatic chooses 1 player (dead or alive). Add the "ballooned" :reminder: to this player. Inform the Demon that the Balloonatic chose the player with "ballooned" reminder. During the night, add a "Demon-killed" :reminder: to each player who the Demon killed. If the Demon ends their ability without "Demon-killed" reminders, the player with "ballooned" reminder is attacked automatically. Add the "Demon-killed" :reminder: if this killed the player. If still no-one got a "Demon-killed" reminder, the Balloonatic is drunk for the whole night (and gets false info). Add the "balloon-drunk" :reminder: to the Balloonatic. Before dawn, wake the Balloonatic again and show them a list of character types. 1 character type for each player with "Demon-killed" reminder (in random order). If the Balloonatic is drunk, show at least one random character type. Do *not* tell the Balloonatic which player has which character type! Remove the "ballooned", "balloon-drunk" and "Demon-killed" :reminder:s at the end of the night.
name: Balloonatic
ability: Each night*, choose 1 player who dies from the Demon if no-one did. The Demon knows who. You see the character type of each (≥ 1) Demon-killed OR are drunk tonight.

firstNightReminder: Invent a kill condition towards 1 player for the Murderer that fits to your setup. Tell the Murderer this condition before the game or in their first night. Note down the condition in your Grimoire.
otherNightReminder: Anytime at day or night, during the game, the Murderer may attempt to satisfy their murder condition by acting towards 1 player according to it. If this happened and the targeted player satisfies the murder condition, the targeted player OR the Murderer is assassinated (i.e. they die even if protected). If assassination is not possible, the attempt is ignored. If this happens at night, the death is only declared at dawn with all the other deaths. If it happens too late in the night, other abilities who get information from deaths might not benefit from the effects of your kill.
name: Murderer
ability: You start knowing a game-related condition. Once per game, anytime, if you attempt to satisfy this condition towards 1 player & succeed, they or you are assasinated.

firstNightReminder: See Other Night Reminder
otherNightReminder: If a 2nd player of the Muezzin's team would have or use a drunk or poisoned ability while already 1 player has one, break the rules in favour of the disadvantaged team or the team of the Muezzin (ideally using the smallest change) such that only 1 player of the Muezzin's team has or uses drunk or poisoned abilities during the whole day or whole night. The Muezzin cannot get drunk (but poisoned).
name: Muezzin
ability: At most 1 player of your team (not yourself) can have drunk or poisoned abilities per night or day.

firstNightReminder: See other Night Reminder.
otherNightReminder: Before the Demon acts, wake the Patroness and let them point at 1 *alive* player (not themself). Sleep. If this player has no outsider ability (i.e. no ability associated to a character in the script's outsider section), add the "blessed" :reminder: to that player. Wishes and state or property changes do not affect the player with "blessed" reminder, if they orignate from a player without "blessed" reminder. (E.g. they don't die from other players.) However, state or property effects may still end regularly (and won't be removed by the blessing). Players with "blessed" reminder are still affected by effects from actions and abilities they use. When the Demon wakes, show them that the Patroness chose the player with the "blessed" reminder.
name: Patroness
ability: Each night, choose 1 alive player without outsider abilities (not yourself) who gets immune against wishes or changes caused by others until dusk. The Demon knows who.

firstNightReminder: Choose 2 players who *actually* have at least 1 pair of different character types among them. (Ignore misregistrations.) Add the "Smarts Yes" :reminder: and "Smarts No" :reminder: to them. Wake the Smartypants and point at the players with "Smarts Yes" and "Smarts No" reminder. Also show the character token of one ability or character of the player with "Smarts Yes" reminder.
name: Smartypants
ability: You start knowing 2 players with different actual character type & 1 actual ability or character among them.

firstNightReminder: See Other Night Reminder.
otherNightReminder: Count the number of players "N" who register as outsiders at dusk and add a "‹N› outsiders" :reminder: to Master Hora. At day, when someone is executed, add a "skip ‹X›" :reminder: to Master Hora where X is the execution's ordinal number (the first execution has X = 0). If X = N, also force the day to end. At dawn, if Master Hora has a "skip ‹X›" reminder where X ≥ 1, replace the "skip ‹X›" reminder with a "skip ‹X-1›" :reminder: and repeat the whole night phase again in correct night phase order. Else, end the night. You might end the night (but not the day) at a dusk (before the next night) if a team has won.
name: Master Hora
ability: Each extra execution swaps the next night with its following day, ≤ current outsider number of times. The game can end only at dusk or dawn.

name: Wicked
ability: Whoever wins, loses & whoever loses, wins, even if you are dead, *except if the Story Teller is executed*.
flavor: But many who are first will be last, and many who are last will be first. (Matthew 30:19)

otherNightReminder: If the Maverick has died, let them choose 1 player. If the chosen player is evil, the Maverick turns into a Heretic next dusk (if good) or next dawn (if evil). At least all players of the opposite team learn about this event.
name: Maverick
ability: When you learn that you died, publicly choose 1 alive player: if they are evil, you *become a Heretic at dusk or dawn and the opposite team learns this*.

firstNightReminder: You can only use the Victim, if there is yet another Outsider in the setup. Wake 1 Minion, show them the 'This character selected you' card and the Victim token. Add the "chases Victim" :reminder: to them in the Grimoire. Wake 1 *genuinely good* Townsfolk, show them the Victim player and character.
otherNightReminder: If the player with "chases Victim" reminder is a minion and guesses a player as Victim publicly today, add the "guessed Victim" :reminder: to the guessed player in the Grimoire (if this player is alive at that moment), otherwise it is put in the center. If a player with Lycanthrope ability chooses the Victim, nothing happens. At (or after) dawn, if the Victim player has the "guessed Victim" reminder, the team of the Victim loses & the other team wins. Remove the "chases Victim" and "guessed Victim" reminders if the Victim character is removed.
name: Victim
ability: If the 1 Minion (who knows you are in play) guesses you (once), your team loses next day. 1 *genuinely good* Townsfolk starts knowing you. [≥ 2 Outsiders]

firstNightReminder: The Prosecutor might be an Ogre or get an evil alignment. If an Ogre, let them choose one player who gets the "friend" :reminder: in the Grimoire (they have equal alignment). The Prosecutor is informed about the alignment they get. Rest: See Other Night Reminder.
otherNightReminder: At day, before executions, take a short pause. During that, the Prosecutor may call their ability to take responsibility for the execution. If the Prosecutor's alignment is equal to the one of the executee, the executee does not die. In this case, add the "drunk" :reminder: to the Prosecutor. While the Prosecutor has the "drunk" reminder, their ability is drunk (which does *not* prevent the Ogre effect). Remove the "drunk" :reminder: when the Prosecutor loses their ability.
name: Prosecutor
ability: Each day, claim prosecution of ≤ 1 executee. If they have your alignment, they don't die & you are drunk from now on. You might be Ogre or evil.
attribution: This character is my derivation of the Laywer idea of Alex from the Werworrias Group! Thank you Alex.

firstNightReminder: Show 1 good bluff character to the Anarchist. It might coincide with the bluff of someone else.
name: Anarchist
ability: You have every alignment & character type unless you die. You register like the interacting player. The demon & story teller choices don't kill you. You get 1 bluff.

firstNightReminder: If the Punk has a drunk Demon ability, the Demon might learn the Punk player. See Other Night Reminder.
otherNightReminder: Play the Punk's drunk ability. At day or night, if a good ability "guesses" the Punk – i.e. an interaction which freely chooses the Punk and whose effect is only *conditional* w.r.t. character (type), alignment or the player state – add the "Punk guess" reminder to them. If someone has the "Punk guess" reminder, choose a player to die preferably in a way that allows for false conclusions regarding a guessing player's ability. If the Punk has a drunk Demon ability, the Demon might learn who the Punk chooses tonight before the Demon chooses.
name: Punk
ability: You have a drunk ability (no Outsider) & register as opposite to it. If you are *guessed* by a good ability, someone *might* die at night. You *might* not register as outsider.

firstNightReminder: Prepare the setup by replacing any 1 other character (and their alignment), not the Demon, with any character and any alignment. At dawn 1 (after the first night), add the "sickened" reminder to the affected player and show the sick character to the Sickener. Wake up the Sickener and show them the sickened player's character.
otherNightReminder: If the Sickener has their ability, don't wake up the player with "sickened" reminder, treat their day actions like a bluff and let their nominations fail covertly or overtly after the ballot with no reason. When the sickened player dies, wake up the Sickener at night. (You may show a death signal.)
name: Sickener
ability: 1 player is sick since 1st dawn. You start knowing their character & learn when they die. You *might* be a Townsfolk. [+1 of any character+alignment]

firstNightReminder: Choose N ≤ 3 actually in-play minion characters and 3 - N minions which are not (and possibly a character that other players believe to be in play). Show these 3 to the Fan. Also: let them choose 1 player for their Ogre ability. They get the chosen player's alignment and are informed about it.
otherNightReminder: At day, when the Fan visited you and made a minion guess: when it was right, they get to see the minion character + evil alignment tonight. Change their character to the minion. Otherwise they get to see a good alignment. Indicate the player's good alignment in the Grimoire by unflipping the Fan token. If they got a new alignment, remove the "Fan Friend" reminder (the Ogre ability is lost).
name: Fan
ability: Once per game, guess 1 of 3 initially seen minion characters to be not in play. If you are right, you get evil + this minion, else you get good. You are an Ogre.

firstNightReminder: If the Gargoyle is part of the majority Team, they are dead from the start. Don't add a Shroud, don't show the death publicly. Otherwise, add a "vivid" :reminder: to the player.
otherNightReminder: If the Gargoyle's team turned from a majority to non-majority, add the "vivid" :reminder: to the player. Don't declare anything. If the Gargoyle is attacked while *not* having the "vivid" reminder, it doesn't kill. If the Gargoyle is killed while having the "vivid" reminder, remove the "vivid" :reminder: & make the Gargoyle die *normally and publicly* & in the next occasion at night, let the Gargoyle choose 1 alive player who dies. If it is an active Demon player, yet another alive player becomes an evil Demon of equal character.
name: Death Gargoyle
ability: You start as covertly dead. Once per game, you get alive if your team loses majority. If you die, choose 1 alive player to kill at night: if a demon, 1 other alive player gets evil+this demon.

firstNightReminder: Decide whether the Bipartisan should be anti the whole game or not. (A normal one is not executed and demon-killed and cannot execute others. An anti-one is assassinated during the game instead of the death of an executee or demon victim but may nominate even if dead, prolonging the game after all goods are dead.) If yes, add the "permanent-anti" :reminder: to the player. Rest: See Other Night Reminder.
otherNightReminder: A Bipartisan with "permanent-anti" reminder has the anti state. If the Bipartisan is anti, they might be assassinated instead of 1 executee or 1 demon victim being killed. Dead or alive players may claim the Bipartisan character at day to call for ballots, no matter their state, but if the player is not an anti Bipartisan, the ballot cannot succeed. The anti Bipartisan's call for a ballot is always treated like a valid call for the ballot and their vote/support for a ballot is always valid and counts positively. On the other hand, normal Bipartisans don't register as players towards any ballot rules, even calls and support for exiles, as long as their vote would count positively. Hence, nominations and votes of normal Bipartisan players covertly fail and get ignored (as if not happened). If a normal Bipartisan is executed or would be killed by the Demon, they don't die. If an anti Bipartisan is in play, the game ends, if only ≤ 2 alive players are not *normal* Bipartisan players (or if remaining good team votes are ≤ evil team votes). If no anti Bipartisan is in play the game ends when only one team is still alive excluding normal Bipartisan players.
name: Bipartisan
ability: You are not Townsfolk & cannot die from executions or Demons & cannot call for & support ballots positively. You *might* be anti.

otherNightReminder: Let the Scaremonger point to a player. If it is a Demon, prompt them to choose again. Move the "Fear" :reminder: next to that player. Declare that a Scaremonger chose a player at dawn. On the final dawn, if a player with "Fear" reminder lives, declare that they die and end the game.
name: Scaremonger
ability: Each night*, you may choose 1 player (no Demon). If you do, this is declared. On the final day, the last player you chose dies, even if you died.

firstNightReminder: Declare that the Panicmonger is in play at dawn. Rest: See Other Night Reminder.
otherNightReminder: The Panicmonger points to a player. Add/Move the "Fury" reminder to that player. Let them point to another player who is good and not a Traveller. Add/Move the "Fear" reminder to that player. If at a day, the player with "fury" executes the player with "fear", show the Grimoire to the Panicmonger at the next night, without any Spy-/Widow-like Jinxes at play.
name: Panicmonger
ability: Each night, choose 1 player, then 1 good non-traveller. If the 1st executes the 2nd with ≥ 5 players alive, evil wins. All players know you are in play.

firstNightReminder: See Other Night reminder.
otherNightReminder: After the demon killed, if a player with "bullied" reminder failed to be publicly mad about being their actual character before the nominations ended, that player dies (if the Bully is sober+healthy at the moment). Now let the Bully choose 1 player. (If the Bully is poisoned or drunk, you may alter their choice arbitrarily.) If the bullied player is good, move the "bullied" :reminder: to them and inform them about being chosen by the Bully.
name: Bully
ability: Each night, ≤ 1 good player is notified, even if you are drunk/poisoned. They die if they are not publicly mad about their actual character before nominations end.

firstNightReminder: See Other Night Reminder.
otherNightReminder: Let them choose 1 player. Move the "whispered" reminder to that player. Players with "whispered" reminder have their final player state effects negated until next dusk (such that they differ from the unnegated state). If they chose themself, their next chosen player will have no dyseffective state until next dusk. It neither changes info they get nor any details or ability rules.
name: Whisperer
ability: Each night, choose a player: they are dyseffective until dusk.

firstNightReminder: See Other Night Reminder.
otherNightReminder: Let them choose 1 player except for themself. Move the "jailed" reminder to that player. Players with "jailed" reminder may not change their player state (they cannot die) and cannot act (no waking up at all, actions at day don't work, nominations fail, they cannot vote except for Travellers).
name: Jailer
ability: Each night, choose 1 player (not yourself): they are captive until dusk.

firstNightReminder: See Other Night Reminder
otherNightReminder: Whenever the Tracker's registered or actual character, alignment or player state affected a player's ability since last dawn, add 1 "tracked" reminder to that player (if there is not already 1). At dawn, show all the players with the "tracked" reminder to the Tracker. Remove the "tracked" reminders.
name: Tracker
ability: Each dawn, see all players (not yourself) whose abilities queried your registered or actual player state or properties correctly since last dawn.

firstNightReminder: Choose a not-in-play Outsider for the Terminator. Wake the Terminator to show them that they might register as that character. Add this character as reminder to the Terminator player.
otherNightReminder: If the Terminator is executed deadly, another alive player of their team gets the Terminator. If the Terminator nominates and executes, such that the Demon ability is removed (e.g. executing the Demon or turning them into a Terminator via self-execution), then the evil team wins. Usually, the Terminator should register as their Outsider: you might choose this way even at the start (preventing the Terminator from getting and being registered by minion info). The Terminator should register normally if no good abilities are in play anymore that could check the evil alignment of the Terminator. If the Terminator resurrects while another one is active, add the "self-poisoned" :reminder: and treat the player as poisoned. while they have this reminder
name: Terminator
ability: If you nominate, execute & remove the Demon, evil wins. If you are executed deadly, 1 alive team member gets Terminator. You *might* register as Outsider. [≥3 evils]

firstNightReminder: See other NIght Reminder.
otherNightReminder: The game does not end, except if someone calls for ending the game and the majority of players agree (like a call for exile). Allow any player to make a call for ending the game. Afterwards, players discuss until they signal their readiness for the ballot. The call and the ballot may happen only once per day. If most people support the call, the game ends and the winners and losers are determined per currently applicable conditions. If you are executed and die AND would lose the game during that moment (evaluating winning conditions for an imagined game end), then the evil team wins.
name: President
ability: The game ends only if the majority votes to end the game. If you are deadly executed while ending the game would make you lose, evil wins.

firstNightReminder: Choose an outsider character's ability for the Tyrant that every Townsfolk should get. Add a "tyrant ‹outsider›" (with the outsider ability) to everyone who registers as Townsfolk to the Tyrant player (even minions if they also register as Townsfolk).  After dawn, you *might* declare that a Tyrant with this outsider ability is in play (for balancing reasons).
otherNightReminder: Take care of the outsider ability of everyone with "tyrant ‹outsider›" reminder.
name: Tyrant
ability: All Townsfolk have an outsider character's ability and *might* register as the ability's character. Everyone or nobody knows which it is.

firstNightReminder: See Other Night Reminder.
otherNightReminder: If a player nominates and has no "nominated" reminder, add the "nominated" :reminder: to them in the Grimoire. If a player has the "nominated" reminder, they are suppressed and you have to reject the nomination.
name: Centralist
ability: Each player (alive or dead) can nominate only once per game.

firstNightReminder: See other Night Reminder
otherNightReminder: At dusk, undo the (last) wish's non-permanent effects for the current player with "cursed" reminder. If it is not the last dusk, let the Sorcerer choose 1 player OR 1 character (but not both, no travellers and not the Sorcerer (player)). Move the "cursed" :reminder: to them. The Sorcerer shows you their prepared wish (or gave it to you at the day). The wish must have ≤ 2 predicates, no AND-like connectives. Only the cursed player can be affected from the wish's effects and may only affect game state or effects that the player triggers by actions and may be conditional. It cannot directly decide about winning or losing of players. You *might* declare the wish towards the player publicly or privately (for balancing). Conditional effects which were declared to the player are permanent when they trigger (even if the trigger condition is gone next dusk). Write down the "‹effect›" :reminder: in the Grimoire for a player who got a permanent effect. Players can only have 1 permanent effect. If they get a new one, the old one is lost. If the Sorcerer dies, remove any "cursed" :reminder:s or "‹effect›" :reminder:s.
name: Sorcerer
ability: Each non-last night, choose 1 player OR character (not yourself) & make a game-related wish (≤ 2 predicates, no AND-like connections) about them which reverts next dusk.

firstNightReminder: Choose 1 player who might be able to nominate more often than all others. Wake the Agitator (if evil) or an evil player and point at this player. Add the "agitated" :reminder: to that player. Rest: See Other Night Reminder.
otherNightReminder: Whenever an evil ability chooses the player with "agitated" reminder, where this choice could possibly kill the player, the Story Teller refuses the choice and prompts to choose again. As soon as there is no player in the game that could nominate & execute as many players as the player with "agitated" reminder has already done, end the game and declare that evil wins (if good has not won by now).
name: Agitator
ability: An evil starts knowing 1 good player who cannot be chosen by evil killing abilities. If this player nominates and executes more *than everyone* else, your team wins.

otherNightReminder: At night 3, wake the Plague and let them choose any Townsfolk or Outsider character (possibly not in the script). The Plague changes to this character (but does not change alignment). Replace the Plague character in the Grimoire with a new instance of the character and flip it down to encode evil alignment (if applicable). If this happens, consider which Townsfolk players should also change (at least 1). If the plague chose a weak character, also turn the actual Townsfolk players into a weaker character. If the plague caused a significant change to the game, multiple Townsfolk may obtain strong characters (which may be duplicates).
name: Plague
ability: At night 3, you change to any chosen Townsfolk character. ≥ 1 Townsfolk player changes to a new Townsfolk character.

otherNightReminder: See First Night Reminder. At night 2 (when the Spin Doctor is or turns evil), wake each of them and inform them about the good character that they register as because of the Spin Doctor. (Also inform the Spin Doctor about their character.) Among all the Spin Doctor's team members, 1 also has the ability of the misregistered character. (This could be the Alchemist player if the Alchemist has the Spin Doctor ability.) Some other team members *might* register as the alignment of their misregistered character instead. No player misregisters as opposite alignment AND has the ability.
name: Spin Doctor
ability: After day 1, all your team's members register as an opposite character. They know which if evil. 1 player of your team has its ability. Other players might register as its alignment.

otherNightReminder: From all supported jinxes in your script, show each to the Hacker whose characters and conditions are satisfied. Now, let the Hacker choose 1 Jinx from the script. (You may note down which.) You change player properties or state in the game such that the chosen Jinx (if previously inactive) now gets active or (if previously active) gets inactive. The Alignment of *characters* must remain. A good character may change to a good character, an evil character may change to an evil character. Evil Minions/Demons don't turn into good. Jinxes and their text are not changed, disabled or enabled. Only the game state changes and activates/deactivates Jinxes. It may also affect other jinxes. If the Jinx cannot be (de)activated this way, the Hacker has no effect.
name: Hacker
ability: Each night*, you see all active jinxes & choose 1 jinx. The story teller changes the game such that this jinx toggles.

firstNightReminder: Devise your setup by starting with only good players and, among them, choose the standard number of good players to become a Legionatic, minus ≥ 0 players who become minions. Choose ≥ 1 Legionatic player to get Legionatic info. Add the "Legionatic" reminder to all Legionatic players. All minions also get it. For the Legionatic Info: Wake up each of them individually (e.g. clockwise). Show them all good players to be good and show them 1 unique not-in-play good character as bluff. Sleep.
otherNightReminder: Choose a player, that player dies. At day, if a nomination gets fake – because no good players voted – treat them as normal but if it gets most of the votes, instead of moving the real "about to die" reminder to it, use the Legionatic's "About to die" reminder and remember the number of votes for both reminders. Later, declare that the player with the real "about to die" reminder is executed.
name: Legionatic
ability: Each night*, 1 player dies. Nominations covertly fail if only evil voted. You have +1 drunk not-in-play ability. Minions & ≥ 1 Legionatic learn the good + 1 bluff. [Most players are Legionatic, ? minions]

firstNightReminder: Remember: all minion characters are poisoned & Pandemon has all poisoned minion characters as well. Give Pandemon only townsfolk bluffs, no outsiders. Immediately after a poisoned minion ability has been used, wake Pandemon, show the Minion ability (character token) and let them act accordingly or give them info. Pandemon registers as any of the characters they have to evade detection and death due to other abilities.
otherNightReminder: Wake Pandemon to kill. Sleep. At night, whenever *a poisoned* (not just drunk) minion has used or would use their ability (but cannot because of their state), wake Pandemon, show the minion ability (character token) and let them act according to it or give info. Sleep. Pandemon has all poisoned minion characters, i.e. Pandemon registers as any of their characters to any ability/rule (arbitrarily) such that it helps Pandemon's longevity (each time at night and day, you analyse and decide what helps them more to not die! It's about life, not winning!) See, if any *good* character was affected or detected by Pandemon's evil alignment, character(s) or character type(s) (even partially, like an Empath getting a 1). If so, before ending the night, wake Pandemon and tell them 1 good character, not the player! Pandemon cannot be, become or register as good, no matter what.
name: Pandemon
ability: Each night*, choose a player to kill & learn 1 character detecting you. Has all poisoned minion characters (arbitrarily registers as minion/demon). You can't be good. [poisoned minions; only townsfolk bluffs]
flavor: I am all evil, even to the evil.

firstNightReminder: After the demon info, let Diabolus choose any players to (mis)register as evil (even Diabolus themself). Add the "register evil" :reminder: to each. Afterwards, let Diabolus choose players to misregister as Leviathan (including Diabolus themself). Add the "register Leviathan" :reminder: to each. Don't let the players know. Characters that must register as specific alignment (e.g. Pandemon) won't be affected by the chosen registration. Sleep. After dawn, declare the Leviathan as you would for a Leviathan game.
otherNightReminder: Wake Diabolus only if a Jinx needs them to choose. It only kills if a Jinx chooses so. Any player with "register evil" reminder registers as evil towards other players. Any player without "register evil" reminder registers as good towards other players. Exception: abilities like Pandemon that overwrite the registration. Any player with "register Leviathan" registers as a Demon player and Leviathan character to abilities. After dawn, declare the Leviathan as you would for a Leviathan game.
name: Diabolus
ability: On your 1st night, choose which players register as evil and which as Leviathan. 1 or any more executions may happen at day, else evil wins. [Leviathan on script]

firstNightReminder: You might use the Loric Gardener to balance the game.
otherNightReminder: Let the Warmonger choose 1 player (dead or alive). Determine the closest evil player (considering only alive players). Start by killing the chosen player then continue to kill the next player towards the next alive evil player until finally the next alive evil player is killed. Ignore all dead players (they are not killed again). If the alive evil player is the Warmonger themself, then they do not die and only good players do.
name: Warmonger
ability: On night ≥ 3, choose 1 player. From this player to inclusively the closest alive evil player, all players die. You cannot kill yourself.

firstNightReminder: If the Loan Shark is a minion, add the "minion" reminder to the player.
otherNightReminder: Let them choose 1 player. If alive, this player gives 1 coin to the Loan Shark. If dead, this player gives 1 vote token to the Loan Shark (which they could only use when dead, e.g. with a Mastermind). If they have no coin: the chosen player dies if the Loan Shark is a demon else (if the Loan Shark is a minion) add 1 "coin debt" or 1 "vote debt" reminder to the chosen player. A player with "coin debt" reminder who earns a coin makes the Loan Shark earn it instead and 1 reminder is removed. Analogously for the "vote debt".
name: Loan Shark
ability: Each night*, choose 1 player: alives give you 1 coin, deads 1 Vote Token. Else: they die OR (if you are minion) owe it to you. The team with most coins wins. You *might* be a minion. [+Shopkeeper]

otherNightReminder: At dusk, if one player deadly executed the Hydra the last day, turn them into a Hydra character with same alignment as before. Also: if there is currently no slayer who can use their ability to kill the Demon, turn the new Hydra player evil & refresh the ability of the alive slayer (telling nobody about it), if a Slayer is alive, else turn an alive *good* player (not the new Hydra player) into the slayer (telling only the new Slayer player about it). If no such alive good player exists to turn into a Slayer, evil wins. The Hydra + Slayer effect does not happen when the Hydra dies without execution. Now at night, wake the Hydra to choose 1 player (not themself). This player dies tonight.
name: Hydra
ability: Each night*, choose 1 player to kill (not yourself). If a player deadly executes you: they get a Hydra & (if not available) a Slayer is added who turns the new Hydra evil.

firstNightReminder: Choose a good character player to get the Twin Demon ability and a good or evil alignment (both of which they get to know). This additional player wakes up directly before or after the original Twin Demon and acts simultaneously with the original Twin Demon player.
otherNightReminder: Let both Twin Demons choose 1 player (but not themself). If they choose the same player (even if both choose the same Twin Demon), nothing happens. Each of them kills the chosen player if the player is alive or resurrects the player if the player is dead. Each Twin Demon cannot kill themself using any of their abilities (including a Boffin ability). At day, if you are executed deadly, your team loses (even if the other Demon lives, even if the other team would have won). If both demons have died without execution, good wins.
name: Twin Demon
ability: Each night*, choose 1 player who dies else resurrects. Your abilities don't kill you. If you are executed deadly, your team must lose. [+1 Twin Demon]

otherNightReminder: Whenever a player (registering as good) sees a *correct association* of the *genuine* player properties (alignment, character or character-type) of the Ker player with *at most half * of the *alive* players (who include the Ker player!), then they have detected the Ker player. Detection is independent of the player realizing or noticing it specifically. Between 1 and every detecting player die tonight (don't blow the cover early). If no-one would die you choose a protected or unprotected player to kill.
name: Ker
ability: Each night*, ≥ 1 *good* player dies who detected your player properties with their ability yesterday or last night, OR 1 player might die.

firstNightReminder: See Other Night Reminder.
otherNightReminder: Let the Steelfzkin choose 1 player to kill. No dead player (except for an anti Bipartisan) can participate in any ballots. This *might* include exile ballots. Any good players' actions that would kill the Steelfzkin do not succeed. During nominations, players may call for guessing 1 player as Demon (if that call was not made already today). Even though dead players cannot vote for it, they might call for a guess or you might reject the call. After a valid call, let them discuss. Cast a ballot if everyone is ready. The guess is accepted only if the ballot succeeds which happens if: the majority of (good or evil) participants or all good participants expressed support AND ≥ 1 good alive player supported the ballot. If no good players can participate anymore in ballots for Demon guesses, the game ends. If a guess is accepted for the Steelfzkin player, then Steelfzkin must die. The guess can only be accepted, if it doesn't target the Steelfzkin player while they cannot die at day. If the target player of an accepted guess is the Steelfzkin, they die and are declared to die. No execution happens after an accepted guess. The day phase ends after an accepted guess without any more nominations and executions.
name: Steelfzkin
ability: Each night*, choose 1 player to kill. Ballots ignore the deads. Good players only kill you if they agree to guess you as Demon today making dusk follow. You register as other Demon.

firstNightReminder: Secretly add the Vice Traveller to the setup. (When declaring the numbers, you might treat the Vice as Townsfolk, Minion or Traveller.) Treat the Vice as a Traveller regarding Minion/Demon info (i.e. the evil Vice only gets to know the Demon but evil players don't know the Vice). Rest: See Other Night Reminder
otherNightReminder: If the Vice has an ability, let them use this ability at the proper time. If this is the last choice that you as story teller will decide by yourself and the Vice player has no reminder "chose", then wake up the Vice player and make them choose for you. You might wake up the Vice earlier in the night to choose for you or if they have the "chose" reminder already. At dawn, remove the "chose" reminder.
name: Vice
ability: At least once each night, you choose in place of the story teller. You are hidden. You register as being a Townsfolk or Minion.

firstNightReminder: Secretly add the Double Agent to the setup (counting as Townsfolk, Traveller or rarely Minion). Choose a principal player of the opposite team & add the "principal" :reminder: to them. Give the good Double Agent a not-in-play Townsfolk, the evil Double Agent a not-in-play Minion. Wake the Double Agent and show their principal player. Wake the principal player and show that a player is their Double Agent. Both might wake up to see each other. Rest: See Other NIght Reminder.
otherNightReminder: If the Double Agent has an ability, let them use this ability at the proper time. If you notice that the principal wishes to make the Double Agent captive, immediately do that. If you are notified or notice that the Double Agent is mainly responsible for the lose of the principal's team, make the Double Agent lose, no matter their alignment or player state.
name: Double Agent
ability: You are hidden & have a principal of opposite alignment. You get captive when they want. If you are most responsible for their team to lose, you lose with them, even if dead. [You *might* be Townsfolk/Minion]

firstNightReminder: Choose a day character and a night character for the Ambivalent. The night character's alignment must fit to the alignment of the Traveller. Rest: See Other Night Reminder.
otherNightReminder: Play the night ability of the Ambivalent at the correct night order time of their ability, if applicable.
name: Ambivalent
ability: You use 2 distinct characters: one at day, one at night. You know which. Your alignment is of the night character. At day, you register as your day character. [You are of any 1 character type]

firstNightReminder: Choose a player state transition for the Traveller. Wake the Traveller and show them the player state transition whose success is inverted. Note down the transition in the grimoire. The states at setup time or at the first dusk are not affected. If you want to protect the Traveller from being killed early, give them a Townsfolk ability (if good) or a Minion ability (if evil) and treat the Prevaricator as a Townsfolk or Minion for various game aspects as you see fit (e.g. you might declare the Traveller character but not the player or replace a setup townsfolk or minion with the Traveller and give them true Minion info). Rest: See Other Night Reminder.
otherNightReminder: If the Prevaricator has an ability, let them use this ability at the proper time. Whenever a player state transition would happen during the game that coincides with the transition of a Prevaricator, this transition is suppressed, if it would have succeeded, and the transition succeeds, if it would have been suppressed. The Prevaricator has no effect on player state while no transition is triggered. The Prevaricator is always exiled, not executed. When the Prevaricator dies, declare the player state transitions which have been inverted by this Traveller, if it was not declared already.
name: Prevaricator
ability: You globally invert the success of specific player state transitions. You know which. All players know which when you die. You *might* be hidden + a Townsfolk/Minion.

otherNightReminder: If no person has the "travel" reminder, let them choose a player (even themself or Travellers). This person quits the game. Add the "travel" reminder to the quit person's token. If quitting would end the game, the game continues and all quit persons rejoin. You might declare the quitting at dawn. They don't count as player anymore. They rejoin at dusk if they are ready and if there are at least 5 other alive non-traveller players. Then remove the "travel" reminder.
name: Travel Agent
ability: Each night*, choose 1 player (even Travellers): they quit the game until the next dusk where ≥ 5 non-Travellers live. You can quit ≤ 1 person at the same time.

firstNightReminder: Choose 1 suitable player (no Traveller) whose alignment is difficult to confirm and often of the same team as the Putschist. Add the "ruler" reminder to the chosen player. Rest: See Other Night Reminder.
otherNightReminder: When the player with "ruler" reminder dies, remove the "ruler" reminder and that player also quits the game (remove the token, they get no Vote Token) and becomes the new Story Teller. Displaced Story Tellers don't revert to players and may not disclose any game info until they are players again. The replacement happens at dawn. Before that, choose another player (like the first time). If the Putschist dies, the original and current Story Teller decide who continues or let the remaining players have a ballot for continuing with the original Story Teller (like an exile ballot). New Story Tellers can be nominated for exile (like a Traveller), but 1 member of each team must support it. If the Story Teller is exiled, the team of the exiled Story Teller loses. Replaced Story Tellers lose Alignment. If a ballot succeeds for a former Story Teller they may receive the penality as specified by the nominator and if exiled, they lose alone and the game continues.
name: Putschist
ability: The Story Teller chooses 1 player. If they die, they quit from now on & displace the Story Teller who chooses again. New Story Tellers can be exiled. Then your team loses.

firstNightReminder: See Other Night Reminder.
otherNightReminder: Get the Spellcaster's wish (in text at night or orally at day). The wish takes effect after the ballot of every valid nomination on the next day. The wish may only concern all nominators, all nominees or all non-travellers (and optionally: all nominees or nominators with successful ballots or failed ones) The wish lasts until the dawn after the nominations. Then all triggered effects by the Spellcaster's wish are rolled back. You might declare the wish at dawn before nominations. You might declare effects explicitly that happen after each nomination. You should declare the wish or the effects, if the wish needs balancing, may affect the end or winning condition (e.g. when players are killed or change alignment) or is dangerous. Invalid nomination/ballots don't trigger spell effects. Wishes cannot directly change ending/winning/losing the game, They cannot alter/break/suppress nominating, executing, ballot success and default effects of these events. The wish cannot reveal private knowledge (i.e. cannot be conditional to properties/state). The wish becomes effectless as soon as it is exploited and violates the constraints. The wish can be reinterpreted to only affect registration towards certain rules/abilities. Ignore wishes that cannot be balanced.
name: Spellcaster
ability: Each night (not the last), you (might) specify state, property or action effects on nominators, nominees or all non-travellers of every nomination until next dawn.

otherNightReminder: If the Careworker is good, move the "patient" :reminder: to the most passive *alive* player yesterday (or during last nominations). Else, if evil, move the "patient" :reminder: to the most active *alive* player. At dusk, wake the Care Worker to show their request or prompt (using text message or arranged gestures) for 1 info signal about the player with "patient" reminder. Only respond with *either* 1 signal, 1 character token *or* 1 pointed player. At night, the patient player is protected from added impairments if the Care Worker is good. Else if evil, ≥ 1 thing about the patient's ability works falsely (different from normally).
name: Care Worker
ability: Each night*, you get 1 answer about the most passive (if good) or most active (if evil) alive player and prevent (if good) or cause (if evil) ability malfunction to them.

firstNightReminder: See Other Night Reminder
otherNightReminder: Let the Suppressor choose 2 players. Add the "suppressed" :reminder: to each of these. At day, if any player with "suppressed" reminder nominates, remove the "suppressed" :reminder:s and let the ballot fail even if the ballot would succeed. At dusk, remove any "suppressed" :reminder:s again.
name: Suppressor
ability: Each night, choose 2 players. The first nomination of any of them fails the ballot.

otherNightReminder: If the Time Eater may eat this night, immediately after you start the night, wake the Time Eater and let them say "yes" or "no". If the Time Eater says "yes", the night suppresses all its effects. End the night without playing any ability. If the Time Eater says "no", continue regularly. If the Time Eater may eat this day, wake them directly before the night ends (at dawn).
name: Time Eater
ability: Each night*, at dusk you *might* decide to skip the night, or at dawn you *might* decide to skip the day.

firstNightReminder: If you want, you can add 1 extra outsider who is Polymath. If you don't, ignore the Polymath night steps. Inform them to be the Polymath. The polymath must be good at setup time. You might change and inform 1 good character player to be evil, preferably an outsider. Choose a fairly compensating not-in-play outsider for the polymath from the script. Show it to the Polymath and put them to sleep. If you think, it's fair for the evil to know, wake the demon and possibly evil players with weak evil value and tell them that the polymath is that outsider. Poisoned minions may get false info. Rest: see Other Night Reminder
otherNightReminder: Present the polymath a script to choose a character from (townsfolk or minion) early in the night. You may disallow choices and prompt to choose again. Disallow any choices that (would) confirm the Polymath player or cannot be bluffed by good/evil players. (For this, remember any Polymath ability choices that you arranged privately at day if applicable.) Sleep. When the Polymath has chosen the N-th ability – where N > current day+night cycle – you might add the "overdriven" (= drunk + deranged) :reminder: to the Polymath player. An overdriven player is either drunk or deranged, whatever helps the opposite team more. Else remove the overdriven :reminder: of the Polymath if applicable. Later in the night, play the chosen character (directly after an equal in-play character would play). Do not allow the Polymath to change the demon character or person and show no grimoire. If the Polymath plays a once-per-game ability, add the "special night" :reminder:. If this reminder is already set, don't allow it. Sleep. Finally after dawn, they might choose an ability sometime at the day, directly in a private talk or indirectly via public claim, which is decided the same way as the choice of the night ability. It might work or not. If the Polymath plays a once-per-game ability, add the "special day" :reminder:. If this reminder is already set, don't allow it.
name: Polymath
ability: 1 good Townsfolk, knowing it, might turn into an outsider+Polymath at night 1: Each night/day, affected by your state, play ≤1 bluffable townsfolk/minion. You register as evil. The evil *might* know your character. +≤1 evil players

firstNightReminder: There are no trust effects.
otherNightReminder: Check that you updated who trusts who during the day by adding the "trusted by" reminder to the trustee player. Do not use "trusted by X" or "treason by X" reminders when the player X has a "trust used" reminder. There is only one "trusted by" and "treason by" reminder per player. If the truster supported the execution for their trustee, e.g. a void vote for the executed trustee, indicate the treason by adding the "treason by X" reminder to the trustee player or remember it. With a "treason" reminder, trusters don't get character+alignment info. But if they get it, add the "trust used" reminder to the receiving player until the end of the game. A player with "trust used" reminder is unconscious except for evil players, i.e. their nomination fails, their vote fails and don't wake up except for seeing their character/alignment change). When the truster actively defends a trustee against execution, remove a treason reminder if applicable.
name: Trustmaker
ability: Anyone who declares trust for a player: your vote doesn't register for their nomination. While alive at their deadly execution (not supported by you), you: learn their private character+alignment & get – or (if evil) misregister as – unconscious from now on.
flavor: My good friend, as we are forced to leave this dire world, I trust you my secret, I am actually your father.

firstNightReminder: If you want, turn 1 minion into the Plotter, add the Plotter token in the Grimoire, otherwise ignore the Plotter's night steps. Count all outsider tokens in the setup. You can add or remove outsiders arbitrarily. Fabled are ignored which restrict outsiders. This number of outsiders is X. Wake the Plotter to choose a character+alignment to register as tonight (regarding abilties AND the game rules). This may affect the minion + demon info step that happens afterwards. First show the minion and demon info. After demon info: Wake the Plotter and show them 1 not-in-play townsfolk and 1 not-in-play outsider (no demon bluffs). Afterwards, let the Plotter choose any players (no Demons & no Travellers) to get a minion character they wish (give no feedback if a wish fails e.g. when the character is already in play or because of your regulation). Wake all outsiders (except the Plotter), let them know the other outsiders and show who is the Plotter. Sleep. For each alignment, wake all minions (except the Plotter), let them know the other minions of equal alignment and show them the Plotter. Sleep. Wake the Plotter, show all final minions (no demons). Sleep. Rest: see Other Night Reminder.
otherNightReminder: At day, if someone validly guesses the Plotter player, the Demon has to choose the Plotter next night. Each Townsfolk & Demons can make 1 valid guess per game. After a valid guess is made, no other guess is valid at the day. There are no wasted guessing attempts. On night X (= setup number of outsiders), first make every townsfolk and demon deranged. If X = 0, it never triggers. Even when the Plotter died, the Plotter chooses to (mis)register their character+alignment after dusk AND before dawn. When the game ends, outsiders, minions and Plotter abilities win, if any Plotter lives OR if the Story Teller was executed. Else, Townsfolk & Demons win. Players lose if their alignment loses. Everyone else has a draw.
name: Plotter
ability: 1 minion, knowing it, might be the Plotter: When the game ends, winning outsiders/minions win if a Plotter lives or if the Story Teller was executed. Otherwise, winning townsfolk/demons win. Other winners have a draw. You choose how to register each dusk & dawn. You get 1 unique Townsfolk + 1 unique Outsider bluff. You may turn ≥ 0 players into minions after demon info. Good minions: know you & each other. Outsiders: know you & each other. If you are validly guessed, the Demon kills you at night. Each Townsfolk+Demon can use 1 public valid guess. Each day, the 1st valid guess is valid. On night X, Townsfolk+demons are deranged until dusk. [X Outsiders]
flavor: I am having a goal! The town of Ravenswood Bluff... WILL BE MINE!!! You shall be fearing me! I HAVE FURY!!

firstNightReminder: Declare that the Dictator is in play. Instead of a Demon, you may add an evil player of any character type to the setup, e.g. an Outsider. If no Demon character is added to the setup, each Minion Info also yields the same 3 character bluffs.
otherNightReminder: Let each alive minion choose ≤ 1 player (individually). Among the most chosen players, you decide the final choice. This player dies (if unprotected). Add the "quit" :reminder: to this player. Players with "quit" reminder cannot interact or act anymore within the game, only communicate, sleep and walk. Only when the game ended, quit persons revert back to players with their most recent state & properties. Only *if no minion is alive*, wake alive Demon players to act tonight. At day, good players are nominated like normally but the condition for a successful ballot changes: ≥ 1 *good+alive* player must vote AND the votes are ≥ 50% of alive players or all alive good players have voted. Else, the ballot for a good player fails. Any executions of good players become exiles. Dead players regain their vote token when the nominee is exiled. Nothing changes with nominations of evil players. When quit persons nominate, the ballot fails even if the success condition is satisfied. If the Dictator would die while ≥ 1 player of minion *type* is alive, the Dictator doesn't die. The good team only wins if they kill the Dictator. The game ends if 1 regular player is left alive (not 2). The evil team (also) may win if ≥ 50% of the alive regular players are minions (except if the evil don't know each other).
name: Dictator
ability: Each night*, alive minions choose 1 player: if good they die+quit. Good players are exiled, not executed. You cannot die if minions live. Good wins if & only if you die. [1 evil of any character]

firstNightReminder: Find a common game-related property that multiple players (at least 2) share. Wake the first player who sees one of those players.
otherNightReminder: At day, allow players to volunteer or use a ballot to determine who should be next. Each player can receive this info only once per game. At night, wake this player or choose one by yourself if no-one was determined. Show 1 player who *currently* shares a common property with the other players when they were pointed at. At least 2 different players are shown until the game ends.
name: Quine
ability: Each night, a new player sees 1 player. The seen players have something in common at the time of seeing them. Players can cast a ballot to decide who gets the next info.

name: Pardoner
ability: Until next dusk, winners of nominations give their character to an unknown player. Afterwards, nomination supporters vote to execute the winner or not.

firstNightReminder: Find a not-in-play character (which also is not a bluff) that you can use as a player. It should be useful or interesting for others to guess and which might let you talk about hypothetical things that you do or experience with this character.
otherNightReminder: At days, allow players to ask a "yes"/"no"-question about your character or ability or else allow them to guess. You may impose an arbitrary (known or unknown) condition or limit of who or how many you allow to guess. Each of them may ask OR guess only once per day. Each day when they guess your character right, you tell about what happened to you as player or what you did.
name: Introvert
ability: You are an impartial player with a not-in-play character (no demon, no traveller). Once per day, certain players may privately ask about or guess it. Who names your ability gets to know what you did or saw last night.

firstNightReminder: You may declare (and explain) some Lorics when players wake up instead of before the first night. When your setup is finished, randomly assign your setup characters to available players. Then you may carry out a sequence of character swaps such that certain players get the character that you want them to have. Now, start waking players according to the night sheet. Wake players first whose ability alters other players' start characters. Wake all players to inform them about their character, even if they would not wake up with their character.
name: Town Planner
ability: Players successively learn their start character when they wake on night 1. The Story Teller or prior night abilities may alter them beforehand. Lorics can be declared on dawn 1.

otherNightReminder: If a player turns evil (e.g. when the old Fang Gu is killed and a new Fang Gu is transfered to an outsider), change the alignment of an evil townsfolk or outsider into good alignment and inform them.
name: Probationer
ability: 1 evil townsfolk/outsider turns good when someone else turns evil.

name: Alien
ability: The character of a Traveller might not be declared.

name: Collaborator
ability: On wakeup, players with minion or demon abilities might get a hint on request (also if drunk) showing ≥ 1 balanced choice. Players still decide on their own.

name: Showmaster
ability: Whenever an outsider effect mentions their team to lose/win, the effect *might* instead turn the outsider into a Heretic and at least the would-be winners learn this.

name: Apatheist
ability: Players (not outsiders) can choose without constraint. The story teller does the rest to fit the selection to the game's requirements.

name: Spoiler
ability: The story teller restricts the script to at most normal size, giving hints about dis- or enabled characters/abilities before the game session. No Story Teller nomination with excluded Atheist.

name: Fair Player
ability: Players die who insult, harass or libel others. Players lose who maliciously hurt or make others cry or disobey game rules. Nominations or kills may not have personal reasons.

name: Repairman
ability: Evils only see each other if ≥ 4 good Townsfolk are in play. ≤ 40% of players can be evil. Social reading & meta gaming is welcome. You don't lie about the game or rules.

name: Anti (State)
ability: All phrases of affected abilities are negated *once* in interpretation such that they always differ from "normal" state. Final info and state effects remain unchanged.

name: Deranged (State)
ability: All final info and state effects of the player's abilities are negated in interpretation, in a detrimental way or at least different from being prudent (not deranged).

name: Sick (State)
ability: Your abilities remain active but you cannot act on them & your nominations fail. Opposite: "fit".

name: Unconscious (State)
ability: You cannot act at day/night (except for exile support) & covertly register as dead. Your state *can* change & your ability remains active.

name: Captive (State)
ability: You cannot act at day/night (except for exile support) & covertly register as dead & your state *cannot* change. Your ability remains active.

name: Quit (State)
ability: You are no player anymore (the game ignores you). This state disappears at latest if the game would end.

name: Suppressed (State)
ability: The affected player's nominations are explicitly rejected by the Story Teller. Opposite: "eligible".

name: Imbecile (State)
ability: The player does not know and is not informed about the character they have or get. It does not affect signals or info obtained from any abilities. Opposite: "rational".

name: Schizophrenic (State)
ability: The player is informed that they have or get false character tokens and possibly a false alignment. Opposite: "judicious"

name: Muted (Condition)
ability: The player uses *game-related* speech acts only in private talks of ≤3 players. (I.e. no calls, nominations or arguments. Game-related statements, insights or commands must be private.) Opposite: "unmuted".

name: Paralyzed (Condition)
ability: The player uses game-related speech acts only at public places or if everyone has gathered. (No private game communication. Uses or simulates no abilities in private.) Opposite: "agile".

name: Accountability (Condition)
ability: Players of a property – who account for something – register to (some) rules as being responsible for an event or effect. E.g. a Demon accounts for a player's death.

name: Multiple Properties
ability: Players may have multiple public or private characters, character types, alignments, states etc. or share some with others. If queried, a player registers according to ≥ 1 of the visible properties they have.
